description: Foreign bank correspondent banking relationship with PATRIOT Act Section 312 certification completed confirming no shell bank status
parsed_text:
CORRESPONDENT BANKING AGREEMENT — DUE DILIGENCE FILE
Institution Name: Banco Nuevo Horizonte SA
Customer Type: Business (Foreign Bank — Correspondent)
Country of Incorporation: Colombia
Swift/BIC: BNHOCOBB2XX
Regulatory Authority: Superintendencia Financiera de Colombia — License No. SFC-BANK-XXXX8821
Principal Address: Calle 72 No. 10-51, Piso 12, Bogotá DC, Colombia
US Contact Phone: +57-1-555-0161   Email: intloperations@nuevohorizonte.example
Business Activity: Retail and commercial banking; trade finance; international wire transfer services
Source of Funds: Customer deposits, interbank placements, trade finance fees
Expected Monthly Activity: $6,800,000

PATRIOT ACT SECTION 312 CERTIFICATION
Anti-Money Laundering Certification received: 2025-09-15
Certified by: Alfonso Rios Castaño, Chief Risk Officer, Banco Nuevo Horizonte SA
Certification confirms:
(a) The bank is NOT a shell bank — it maintains a physical presence in Colombia at the registered address above as evidenced by its SFC license and published audited financial statements.
(b) The bank is NOT providing correspondent services to shell banks.
(c) The bank is subject to AML/CFT supervision by the Superintendencia Financiera de Colombia.

OWNERSHIP
Beneficial Owner: Nuevo Horizonte Financial Group SAS — 72% controlling shareholder (Colombian holding company, Bogotá Stock Exchange listed). No natural person holds 25%+ directly. Regulated entity / publicly listed exemption documented.
Control Person: Luciana Vargas Mendez, CEO

SCREENING RESULTS
OFAC / SDN: Clear — no match.
UN Consolidated Sanctions List: Clear.
US State Department INCSR Colombia Country Report: Colombia remains FATF-monitored jurisdiction. High-risk country designation applied per internal policy — EDD mandatory for all Colombian correspondent accounts.
PEP Status: Luciana Vargas Mendez — no PEP flag.
Adverse Media: One 2021 news reference regarding industry-wide SFC inquiry into trade finance practices; Banco Nuevo Horizonte not named. Cleared — no adverse finding for this entity.
Wolfsberg Group Correspondent Banking Due Diligence Questionnaire: Completed and returned 2025-08-30.

RISK ASSESSMENT AND EDD ACTIONS
EDD Trigger: Yes — Colombia high-risk jurisdiction classification.
1. Wolfsberg DDQ reviewed and assessed as satisfactory.
2. SFC license and annual financial statements (2024) reviewed.
3. PATRIOT Act Section 312 certification filed.
4. Payable-through account assessment: Not applicable.
5. Senior management approval: Granted.
PEP Status: No
Risk Rating: High
CDD Level: Enhanced
Review Date: 2025-11-28   Reviewer: Monique Delacourt

expected_json.occupation_or_business: Retail and commercial banking; trade finance; international wire transfer services
expected_json.source_of_funds: Customer deposits, interbank placements, trade finance fees
